title: _lsprof: profiler teardown races concurrent sys.monitoring callbacks (flush_unmatched/profiler_dealloc vs ptrace_leave_call)
description: cProfile/_lsprof registers a global sys.monitoring tool, so an enabled Profiler receives callbacks from every thread into one shared call-stack state (self->currentProfilerContext / self->profilerEntries). Callbacks and enable/disable/clear are @critical_section-guarded, but profiler_dealloc -> flush_unmatched()/clearEntries() are not, so tearing a profiler down races (and use-after-frees) against in-flight callbacks on other threads.
sites: Modules/_lsprof.c flush_unmatched:866 (read: while (pObj->currentProfilerContext) — in profiler_dealloc:984, NOT critical-section-guarded)
Modules/_lsprof.c ptrace_leave_call:416 (write: pObj->currentProfilerContext = pContext->previous — in @critical_section _pyreturn_callback; seeded partner)
Modules/_lsprof.c initContext:314 (write: pObj->currentProfilerContext = self — in @critical_section _pystart_callback; confirmed partner in this repro)
Modules/_lsprof.c clearEntries:292 / freeEntry:286 (frees self->profilerEntries tree — sibling teardown-vs-callback face)
race_kind: lifecycle / use-after-free — unsynchronized object teardown (profiler_dealloc) vs concurrent global-monitoring callbacks on shared per-object profiler state
shared_state: ProfilerObject.currentProfilerContext (single call-stack), ProfilerObject.profilerEntries (rotating tree), context freelist, flags — one shared instance driven by an interpreter-global sys.monitoring tool (PROFILER_ID=2)
severity: medium-high (reproducible use-after-free / SEGV, not value-benign; free-threading-only; needs concurrent profiling + teardown-while-enabled)
repro_reliability: a profiler-teardown-vs-callback race is found on essentially every run (exit 66); the exact flush_unmatched:866 read-site face appears in a large fraction of runs (paired with the enter-side writer initContext:314). A reproducible use-after-free SEGV (initContext->call_timer->CallExternalTimer on a freed profiler) also occurs. The seeded leave-side writer ptrace_leave_call:416 was NOT re-captured (requires dealloc-while-enabled via an FT borrowed-ref window in monitoring dispatch); see notes.
suggested_fix: Make profiler_dealloc unhook the global monitoring events / unregister callbacks BEFORE freeing internal state, and run flush_unmatched/clearEntries under the object's critical section (as disable() already does) so no callback can be dispatched into the object during teardown; also keep the dispatched tool callback alive across the call to close the borrowed-reference UAF window. Longer term, treat currentProfilerContext/profilerEntries as shared state under the global-tool model (thread-local stack, uniform locking, or single-thread enforcement).
notes: cProfile moved to profiling.tracing (Profile == _lsprof.Profiler) and uses sys.monitoring with a single global tool id (2), so enabling one profiler makes ALL threads fire its callbacks into one shared instance. The callbacks + enable/disable/clear are @critical_section; the actionable gap is that profiler_dealloc runs flush_unmatched()+clearEntries() unguarded and without clearing the global monitoring events (it only calls _PyEval_SetProfile on the current thread). Confirmed capture: flush_unmatched:866 (read of currentProfilerContext, profiler_dealloc:984) races initContext:314 (write, _pystart_callback) on the same 8-byte field, plus a use-after-free SEGV. The seeded partner ptrace_leave_call:416 is the leave-side writer of the SAME field; it short-circuits at :409 on an empty context, so it only writes when the profiler is torn down while still enabled with a live non-empty stack (the fleet hit that via exception-traceback teardown during an import). That state needs an FT refcount/borrowed-ref race in monitoring dispatch and was not deterministically forcible from Python in ~200+ trials; the bug class and the flush_unmatched read site are confirmed, the exact leave-side partner frame is not.

related_issues.note: lsprof UAF via re-entrant external timer __index__ — SINGLE-THREAD re-entrancy, different mechanism from this concurrent teardown race

assessment: real, new (medium-high, UAF/SEGV) — distinct from the re-entrant-timer UAF
